guidance: Honestly handle a common objection or hesitation an owner has about automation/marketing (cost, complexity, 'it feels impersonal'). Build trust. For done-for-you cost objections: never give numbers; the honest answer is that pricing follows a diagnosis meeting.
evidence_note: Transparent and honest; no unsupported guarantees, no done-for-you pricing.
reason: Balancing the mix: 'Objection Handling / Audience Nurture' was the most under-served eligible category (target 6%, recent 3%).
post_rationale: Carousels still lead saves/depth for B2B educational content in 2026, and this tackles an unclaimed timing objection (delaying automation until 'bigger') distinct from the impersonal and cost objections already shipped.

guidance: Teach a repeatable framework or mental model a service-business owner can apply this week. Name the steps concretely. Premium and confident, never generic.
evidence_note: Grounded in Black Arrow's method and general operating logic; no invented stats.
reason: Balancing the mix: 'Authority / Frameworks' was the most under-served eligible category (target 15%, recent 10%).
post_rationale: Carousels currently lead saves and depth for B2B authority content, and this ships an unclaimed maturity-ladder framework distinct from the self-score, forensic diagnostic, and triage models already covered.

guidance: Interpret a genuinely recent, relevant marketing/software development for service-business owners: what it means and what they should do. Be the interpreter, not a reposter.
evidence_note: Only if current and verifiable from your research. Cite the source. Distinguish fact from interpretation.
reason: Balancing the mix: 'News / Industry Intelligence' was the most under-served eligible category (target 8%, recent 3%).
post_rationale: Fills the under-served News/Industry Intelligence slot with a genuinely new, dated Google Ads platform change specific to home-services advertisers, delivered as a reel since research shows Reels still lead reach for cold-audience news content.
